faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Elizabeth home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, webbing in closet corners, silken tubes or cases on fabrics, and seeing small beige or gold-colored moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae (small white caterpillars) on clothing or carpet edges. Our Elizabeth technicians provide free inspections to confirm the extent of any infestation.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control service include?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive clothes moth control includes a thorough inspection of closets, storage areas, and upholstered furniture, identification of infestation sources, targeted treatment using EPA-approved products, removal of larvae and eggs, and preventive measures. We also provide guidance on cleaning affected items and preventing future infestations. Follow-up visits are included in most treatment plans to ensure complete elimination.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth treatment cost vary from $550 to $5,000 in Elizabeth?
faqs.answer: Treatment costs depend on the size of your home or business, severity of the infestation, number of affected rooms, and the extent of treatment required. A small infestation in a single closet may cost around $550, while treating an entire multi-story Elizabeth home with extensive fabric damage and multiple treatment visits can reach $5,000. We provide detailed estimates after our initial inspection.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to clothes moth problems in Elizabeth, NJ?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service to Elizabeth residents in most cases. Because clothes moths can cause significant damage to expensive garments and textiles, we prioritize rapid response. Our proximity to Elizabeth allows us to quickly dispatch technicians to assess your situation and begin treatment, minimizing further damage to your belongings.
